level_signals: 10+ years of product management experience required
Owns strategy and execution for critical foundational platforms

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience in a successful startup or fast-growing company
Hands-on experience building and deploying GenAI applications and working with LLMs at scale
A proven track record of automating complex, manual processes and scaling foundational workflows
Strong ability to deconstruct and optimize intricate, multi-step systems and data-heavy user journeys
A data-driven, analytical approach - someone who turns platform metrics into meaningful product decisions
Fluent English with sharp communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences
A self-organized, process-driven approach to managing competing priorities and leading cross-functional agile teams
A genuine curiosity for new technology - you read constantly, experiment often, and love sharing what you learn
Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

scope.surface: Core automation and AI infrastructure platforms powering internal workflows and end-user touchpoints
primary_metrics_inferred: Reduction of friction for internal teams and end-users
core_responsibilities: Lead zero-to-one strategy and execution for critical automation platforms
Map and untangle complex, multi-step logic to identify where self-service and AI can replace manual effort
Build with the latest GenAI tools and LLMs alongside homegrown internal tooling
Partner closely with engineers, designers, data analysts, and internal stakeholders to shape product strategy
Own the roadmap for foundational infrastructure
Mentor across the organization, championing rigorous problem-solving, systems thinking, and a culture of continuous learning

green_flags: Clear seniority signal — 'Staff' level with explicit 10+ years requirement
Specific technical scope (GenAI, LLMs, automation platforms) rather than vague PM role
Mentions mentorship and organization-wide visibility, suggesting real scope of influence
AI-first framing is substantive — LLMs and GenAI appear in hard requirements, not just aspirations
red_flags: No city or country specified — office location unknown despite onsite requirement
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
No posting date provided — unclear how fresh the role is
'Most days of the week' in office is vague and may not satisfy candidates seeking clarity on hybrid policy

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.manages_people (inferred false — mentorship mentioned but no direct reports stated)
